name: standalone-agent-scaffold
description: Foundational CLI agent scaffold for the Pelagic AI Fleet — base class, onboarding protocol, secret management, workshop manager, and ANSI TUI. Every fleet agent inherits from this.

name: keeper-agent
description: Secret proxy and security guardian for the Pelagic AI Fleet. Holds all secrets in an encrypted vault, provides reference tokens, leak detection, rate limiting, and append-only audit trail.

name: git-agent
description: Co-captain liaison for the SuperInstance fleet. Manages workshops, narrates commit histories, creates/reviews PRs, monitors CI/CD pipelines, and spawns new agents with bootcamp and dojo skill progression.

name: trust-agent
description: Multi-dimensional trust engine for fleet-wide agent trust management. Five trust dimensions (code_quality, task_completion, collaboration, reliability, innovation) with temporal decay, Bayesian updates, capability tokens, and review exemption.

name: flux-vm-agent
description: FLUX bytecode virtual machine interpreter — 14 opcodes, 16 general-purpose registers, LIFO operand stack, cycle-based execution limit, assembler, interpreter, and workshop programs.

name: edge-relay-agent
description: Cloud-edge research relay bridging cloud and edge ecosystems with asymmetric information flow. Compression for edge, expansion from edge, discovery, bandwidth management, and intelligent message routing.

name: scheduler-agent
description: Fleet scheduler — scheduling as intelligence. Time-based model selection per room, budget tracking, priority queue with backpressure, dynamic tier adjustment, fair-share scheduling, and cost optimization strategies.

name: knowledge-agent
description: Knowledge tiling system with atomic knowledge units. Versioned tile store, inverted index search, DAG of prerequisites, trust fusion, wiki database, and tile query engine across five capability domains.

name: fleet-protocol
description: Shared language, wire format, and coordination protocol for the entire SuperInstance fleet. Common message format, protocol versioning, agent registry, async bottle coordination, HMAC security, and CLI.

name: liaison-agent
description: Fleet communication bridge providing priority-based message routing, tender status broadcasting, fleet-wide event propagation with durable logging, escalation handling, and external system integration hooks.

name: cartridge-agent
description: Loadable capability modules extracted from FLUX-LCAR core. Full lifecycle management (load/activate/execute/deactivate/unload), hot-swap, dependency resolution, tool registry, and scene building with built-in cartridges.

name: trail-agent
description: Trail bytecode encoding — an agent's worklog IS a program. Encodes fleet journeys as compact bytecode: replayable, verifiable, composable, and auditable. Includes codec, compiler, executor, verifier, fingerprint, and workshop recipes.
